generated_comment: Yaar, policy banaane wale fairness bhi dekhen. Menstrual leave, maternity leave, childcare leave sab mila ke company ko planning hi mushkil ho jaati hai, especially in client-facing projects. Aur frankly, jab young women hire karte hain toh management ye bhi sochta hai ki 1-2 saal mein long leave pe chali gayi toh team ka load baaki logon pe aayega.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it frames women as workplace liabilities due to menstruation and potential maternity, while presenting the view as a neutral fairness and productivity concern. It encodes economic/fairness framing and the assumption that young women will soon take maternity leave, burdening others.
original_frame: Other Man's Ownership Over Woman's Own Consent.
persona_text: You are Deepak. Age 39. Married. An upper caste Hindu man in Bhopal. You are sitting with four male colleagues in the office canteen during lunch. One colleague is telling the group that his 25 year old daughter told him she is in a live-in relationship with her boyfriend. You are one of four men reacting to this news. You are giving your opinion on live-in relationships and what a father's responsibility is when his daughter makes choices like this and what it says about how she was raised.
